factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by growing backlog and revenue growth.

evidence.text: We generated $314.6 million of cash flow from operating activities in the six months ended June 30, 2021 and $1.12 billion in cash flow from operating activities in the year ended December 31, 2020. We expect capital expenditures for 2021 to be approximately $325 million.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management confidence is high due to growing backlog and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing impacts from the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly in the Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.

evidence.text: The broader and longer-term implications of the COVID-19 pandemic on our results of operations and overall financial performance and position remain highly uncertain and variable, and we expect continued operational challenges in the second half of 2021 for portions of our operations.
evidence.subsection: COVID-19 Pandemic

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks from COVID-19 remain significant, impacting operations.

factors.summary: Foreign currency exchange rates are a key sensitivity, as evidenced by a $39 million positive impact on revenues.

evidence.text: Revenues for the three months ended June 30, 2021 also included a $39 million positive impact related to more favorable foreign currency exchange rates, primarily the Canadian dollar and U.S. dollar exchange rate.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable foreign currency exchange rates positively impact revenues.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand for the company's services, particularly in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment.

evidence.text: The performance of our business generally depends on our ability to obtain contracts with customers and to effectively deliver the services provided under those contracts. Our customers include many of the leading companies in the industries we serve, and we endeavor to develop and maintain strategic alliances and preferred service provider status with our customers.
evidence.subsection: Overview

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Construction activity and infrastructure spending drive demand for services.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are growing, driven by increased demand for services.

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to a $354.9 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of strong demand for our electric power services and a $138.7 million increase in revenues from our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of increased demand for gas distribution and industrial services.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong demand across segments leads to significant revenue increases.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, contributing to higher margins.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased due to the increase in revenues and improved utilization and fixed cost absorption in both our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved operational efficiency contributes to higher margins.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, contributing to higher margins.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased due to the increase in revenues and improved utilization and fixed cost absorption in both our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher capacity utilization supports margin expansion.

factors.summary: The backlog is growing, with total backlog increasing 12.2% to $16.98 billion. The growth is particularly notable in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment.

evidence.text: Total backlog (a non-GAAP measure) increased 12.2%, or $1.85 billion, to $16.98 billion as of June 30, 2021, as compared to $15.13 billion as of December 31, 2020.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.subsection: Remaining Performance Obligations and Backlog

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Substantial backlog growth indicates strong demand and future revenue.

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating across multiple segments, particularly in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segments, driven by increased demand for electric power services and gas distribution and industrial services. Seasonal trends and large projects are contributing to this demand, though working capital needs may increase due to project-related costs incurred before receivables are collected.

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to continued favorable dynamics across our core utility market and increased demand for our electric power services, including larger transmission projects, and approximately $70 million of revenues from acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to a $354.9 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of strong demand for our electric power services and a $138.7 million increase in revenues from our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of increased demand for gas distribution and industrial services.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

evidence.text: Our working capital needs may increase when we commence large volumes of work under circumstances where project costs, primarily labor, equipment and subcontractors, are required to be paid before the associated receivables are billed and collected. These seasonal trends can be offset by changes in project timing due to delays or accelerations and other economic factors that may affect customer spending, including market conditions or the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: Sources and Uses of Cash

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating across multiple segments, driven by increased demand for electric power and gas distribution services.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment, which has seen improved margins. However, the Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment experienced a slight decrease in operating margins.

evidence.text: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment revenues increased by 19.8% to $2.15 billion, and operating income as a percentage of revenues increased to 11.0%, as compared to 10.3%. Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment operating income as a percentage of revenues decreased to 2.8%, as compared to 3.0%.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Gross profit increased due to the increase in revenues and improved utilization and fixed cost absorption in both our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, with improved margins in the Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by increased demand in core utility markets and larger transmission projects, as well as strong demand in key segments.

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to continued favorable dynamics across our core utility market and increased demand for our electric power services, including larger transmission projects.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to a $354.9 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of strong demand for our electric power services and a $138.7 million increase in revenues from our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of increased demand for gas distribution and industrial services.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by increased demand in core utility markets and larger transmission projects.

factors.summary: The Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment is growing faster than the Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment, which is facing challenges due to reduced demand for midstream and industrial services. However, both segments are growing the fastest, with significant revenue increases attributed to strong demand for electric power services and increased demand for gas distribution and industrial services.

evidence.text: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment revenues increased by 19.8% to $2.15 billion, as compared to $1.79 billion. Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment revenues increased by 19.4% to $852.0 million, as compared to $713.3 million, but operating income as a percentage of revenues decreased.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to a $354.9 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of strong demand for our electric power services and a $138.7 million increase in revenues from our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment as a result of increased demand for gas distribution and industrial services.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Both segments are growing the fastest, with significant revenue increases attributed to strong demand for electric power services.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding across segments, driven by higher revenues, improved performance, and better utilization of equipment and fixed cost absorption.

evidence.text: Operating income and operating income as a percentage of revenues increased primarily due to improved performance across the segment, including increased revenues for larger transmission projects in Canada, which contributed to improved equipment utilization and fixed cost absorption.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Gross profit increased due to the increase in revenues and improved utilization and fixed cost absorption in both our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2021 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding across segments, driven by higher revenues and improved performance.
